title: Force unwrap on network-derived uint32 in loadBinaryMessage
description: At line 460, messageIDData.uint32! is force-unwrapped. While the previous guard at line 453 checks messageIDData.uint32 for nil, the force unwrap at line 460 is a separate call. If the .uint32 computed property is non-deterministic or if the data is mutated concurrently, this could crash. More critically, at line 487, fieldLengthData.uint32! is force-unwrapped without any prior nil check whatsoever. If the uint32 property returns nil (e.g., insufficient bytes), this is a guaranteed fatal crash from network input.
recommendation: Replace force unwraps with guard-let: guard let fieldLengthValue = fieldLengthData.uint32 else { return } and reuse the already-validated value from line 453 instead of re-reading.
notes: Already fixed: loadBinaryMessage now uses guard-let for all uint32 conversions
